concerns.text: T022 designates the Wilcoxon signed-rank test as the PRIMARY method and relegates the paired t-test (mandated by FR-006 and SC-003) to an 'optional' robustness check. This is a semantic weakening of the functional requirement; the task must implement the paired t-test as the primary statistical test to satisfy FR-006.

T010 requires raising a `PCEAnomalyError` and halting the pipeline if PCE values deviate from expected ranges. This violates Constitution Principle III (Data Hygiene) which mandates flagging anomalies for review without modifying or rejecting raw data. The task must log anomalies to a review queue and proceed, not fail the run.

T002 initializes the project with Python 3.10, but the plan.md (Technical Context) and Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility) require Python 3.11 to match pinned dependency versions and the target GitHub Actions runner environment. This creates a constraint violation regarding the execution environment.

T019 describes a 'fixed duration' timeout wrapper but fails to explicitly enforce the 6-hour limit or the specific 'Time Limit Exceeded' status code required by the spec's Edge Cases section. This is a silent relaxation of the hard constraint defined in the spec.
